title: jodrellia fistulosa
intro: Jodrellia fistulosa is a plant species commonly known as the "corkwood" or "corkwood tree," belonging to the Ebenaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in its bark, which contains a high concentration of 19-hydroxy-alpha-lanosterol, a compound with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits associated with Jodrellia fistulosa include its potential to treat various ailments, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its ability to act as an antiseptic and antibacterial agent. In traditional herbal preparations, Jodrellia fistulosa bark is often dried and crushed to make a powder or tincture, which can be used to treat a range of health issues.
